texto: Sam was your brother, and Trick was your friend, but what they did had nothing to do with you. You don't have to choose. Just because me and your dad couldn't live together, didn't mean you had to stop loving one of us, did it? Doesn't work like that. Love doesn't work like that.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a C.J. Flood: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “Sam was your brother, and Trick was your friend, but what they did had nothing to do with you. Yo…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amizade, death, grief pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de luto e termino.

frase_original: Sam was your brother, and Trick was your friend, but what they did had nothing to do with you. You don't have to choose. Just because me and your dad couldn't live together, didn't mean you had to stop loving one of us, did it? Doesn't work like that. Love doesn't work like that.
